message.content: Adversaries may exploit Microsoft Exchange transport agents to maintain persistent access to systems. These agents, which can be custom-developed and registered with the Exchange server, operate on email messages during the transport pipeline to perform tasks like spam filtering or adding signatures. Malicious transport agents can be configured to trigger specific actions based on adversary-defined criteria, such as copying attachments for exfiltration when certain email addresses are detected. This technique allows adversaries to persist within an Exchange environment and potentially exfiltrate sensitive information. Common exploitation methods include registering a malicious transport agent that activates during email processing stages. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring and auditing transport agent registrations, employing strict access controls, and regularly updating and patching Exchange servers to prevent unauthorized modifications.
